sourceMovie.title: Summer Wars
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Summer Wars works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Paprika
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Summer Wars clicked for you, Paprika (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Summer Wars?
faq.answer: Try Paprika, Belle, Stand by Me Doraemon—each shares DNA with Summer Wars while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Summer Wars?
faq.answer: Summer Wars (2009) is best described as Animation, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Summer Wars worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
